dimension_notes.naming_quality: Consistent kebab-case file naming throughout. PascalCase component files renamed to kebab-case. Variable and function names are descriptive. fetchAndSet helper follows verb+noun convention. Factory function names remain method-centric but functional. Zod schema names follow clear pattern (channelPatchSchema, webhookCreateSchema, webhookMessageSchema).
dimension_notes.logic_clarity: Channel PATCH handler replaced 7-field sequential validation chain with zod schema. Webhook route handlers now use zod for body validation instead of manual type checks. Streaming handlers use extracted pure helpers. Route handler logic uses clear early returns. fetchAndSet eliminates repetitive branching in refresh functions.
dimension_notes.type_safety: Three route handlers now use zod schemas for runtime validation (channel PATCH, webhooks POST, webhook message POST). Gateway-client.ts has typed payload interfaces. Pure helper functions have explicit input/output types. Webhook typed message validation uses z.enum for valid types. Remaining route handlers use simpler bodies that don't need schema validation.
dimension_notes.contract_coherence: PROTOCOL.md is comprehensive and up to date. Zod schemas in channel and webhook routes make contracts explicit and self-documenting. Typed payload interfaces in gateway-client.ts. Webhook message schema validates content as union type (string | object) matching actual usage.
dimension_notes.error_consistency: All route handlers use consistent [module] prefix pattern for console.error logging. DELETE endpoints consistently return {success: true}. Error responses follow {error: string} pattern. Zod validation returns first error message for clear client feedback. Security-sensitive error messages no longer leak secret names.
dimension_notes.abstraction_fitness: fetchAndSet helper eliminates 3 repetitive fetch-parse-set patterns in ChatProvider. Handler registration pattern well-extracted. Pure streaming helpers exported for direct testing. mergeServerData provides clean state update abstraction. Zod schemas encapsulate validation logic at route boundaries.
dimension_notes.ai_generated_debt: fetchAndSet helper eliminated the most glaring copy-paste pattern (refreshChannels/refreshMembers/refreshAgents). Zod schemas replaced hand-rolled validation in 3 route handlers. Previously repetitive error logging standardized with [module] prefix. VALID_TYPED_TYPES extracted from inline array to const. Webhook body validation consolidated.
dimension_notes.high_level_elegance: Clean separation: Gateway=transport, Go=orchestration, Web=API+UI. Package boundaries are well-defined. No circular dependencies between packages. Zod validation aligns with modern Next.js API patterns. Runtime validation at API boundaries, TypeScript types for internal contracts.
dimension_notes.mid_level_elegance: ChatProvider significantly simplified with fetchAndSet helper and consistent mergeServerData usage. Streaming lifecycle well-structured with exported pure helpers. Webhook route handlers cleaned up with zod schemas replacing manual validation. Some components still manage multiple concerns but complexity is genuine.
dimension_notes.low_level_elegance: Zod schemas eliminate inline type assertions in channel PATCH and both webhook routes. Pure helper functions exported for direct testing. Variable renames improve clarity. No remaining inline type casts in modified route handlers. Content union type (string | object) properly handled in webhook message handler.
dimension_notes.cross_module_architecture: Clear package boundaries. Gateway client abstracts HTTP transport cleanly. CLI package properly isolated. Shared types package keeps common interfaces DRY. Zod schemas colocated with route handlers they validate.
dimension_notes.initialization_coupling: Environment variable reads are at call-time. Module-level state is minimal. PrismaClient uses globalThis singleton correctly. fetchAndSet is a pure function with no module-level dependencies. mergeServerData is a pure function.
dimension_notes.convention_outlier: File naming consistently kebab-case throughout. Error logging uses uniform [module] prefix pattern. Test files import from real modules rather than re-implementing helpers. Zod schema naming follows consistent pattern (entityActionSchema). Mock patterns still vary slightly across test files.
dimension_notes.dependency_health: Dependencies well-managed. No circular imports. Package boundaries clean. Zod properly used for runtime validation in 3 route handlers. Phoenix JS dependency should still be version-pinned.
dimension_notes.test_strategy: 395+ tests across 36+ test suites. Pure helpers tested via real exported functions (use-channel helpers, fetchAndSet, mergeServerData). New chat-provider-helpers.test.ts covers state management and data fetching patterns. Channel PATCH zod schema tested through route handler tests. Coverage gaps narrowing but UI components still lack direct tests.
dimension_notes.api_surface_coherence: DELETE endpoints standardized. Route handler signatures consistent. Channel PATCH and webhook routes use zod schemas for explicit contracts. Webhook message schema validates content as string|object union. Some list endpoints still return arrays while others wrap in named properties.
dimension_notes.authorization_consistency: Auth patterns consistent: session check, permission check, then action. Admin token authentication uses timing-safe comparison. Webhook token auth via URL parameter follows Discord convention. Error logging for auth failures uses consistent format. Owner-only checks could be more unified.
dimension_notes.incomplete_migration: PascalCase to kebab-case migration complete including typed-messages components. All .js files migrated to .ts. Zod validation migration progressing (3 of the most complex route handlers now use zod). Sequence type handling still varies between string and bigint in some places.
dimension_notes.package_organization: Clean package structure: web, shared, cli, gateway, streaming. Auth files follow consistent naming. Components well-organized in feature directories. Test files colocated with source via __tests__ directories. PascalCase files renamed to kebab-case.
dimension_notes.design_coherence: Consistent patterns for route handlers, streaming lifecycle, and broadcast helpers. State management consolidating toward fetchAndSet + mergeServerData patterns. Zod validation establishes a clear validation pattern at API boundaries. Three route handlers now follow the same zod-parse-then-handle pattern.

issues.suggestion: Add component tests for critical UI components using testing-library.
issues.summary: UI components lack direct unit tests despite having pure helpers tested
evidence: While lib modules and pure helpers now have good test coverage (36+ suites, 395+ tests), React component behavior is not directly tested. The ChatProvider is tested via its pure helpers but component lifecycle and rendering are not.

issues.suggestion: Pin phoenix dependency to exact version to prevent unexpected WebSocket protocol changes.
issues.summary: Phoenix JS client dependency should be version-pinned
evidence: The phoenix package dependency uses a semver range rather than an exact version lock.

issues.suggestion: Standardize list response shape to always wrap in a named property.
issues.summary: List endpoints have inconsistent response shapes
evidence: Some list endpoints wrap results in {members: [...]} while others return bare arrays.

issues.suggestion: Standardize sequence type handling to always use string representation.
issues.summary: Sequence values are sometimes numbers, sometimes strings across the codebase
evidence: fetchChannelSequence returns string but some callers and DB models expect bigint.

issues.suggestion: Consider domain-intent names if touching these functions in future.
issues.summary: Route handler factory functions use method-centric naming
evidence: Functions like createInternalMessagesPostHandler describe HTTP method rather than domain action.

issues.suggestion: Add runtime validation for gateway broadcast payloads or document shapes in PROTOCOL.md.
issues.summary: Gateway broadcast contract is only implicitly defined through TypeScript interfaces
evidence: Broadcast payload shapes are defined in TypeScript but not validated at runtime against the Elixir gateway.

issues.suggestion: Consider splitting HandlerDeps into narrower interfaces per handler group.
issues.summary: HandlerDeps interface bundles many unrelated concerns
evidence: HandlerDeps bundles refs, setters, and utility functions into one interface making it unclear which handler uses which deps.

issues.suggestion: Look for additional DRY opportunities in route handler error handling.
issues.summary: Some repetitive try/catch/error-response patterns remain across route handlers
evidence: While the biggest copy-paste patterns are now DRY, similar try/catch/return-error patterns exist across route handlers that could benefit from a shared error wrapper.

issues.suggestion: Consider a unified isOwner check or ensuring owner always has all permissions.
issues.summary: Owner-only checks vary in implementation across endpoints
evidence: Server DELETE checks server.ownerId directly while other endpoints use checkMemberPermission.

issues.suggestion: Establish a standard mock pattern and document it in a testing guide.
issues.summary: Test files use slightly different mocking approaches
evidence: Some tests use vi.stubGlobal, others use vi.mock with factory functions, and some use inline mocks.

issues.suggestion: Extract gateway URL construction into a config module.
issues.summary: Web package constructs gateway internal API URLs directly
evidence: gateway-client.ts constructs URLs like /api/internal/broadcast directly, coupling to gateway URL structure.

issues.suggestion: Consider extending fetchAndSet pattern to refreshPermissions for full consistency.
issues.summary: refreshPermissions still uses manual fetch pattern instead of fetchAndSet
evidence: refreshPermissions handles its own fetch/parse/error cycle rather than using the new fetchAndSet helper, since it needs special bigint conversion logic.

issues.suggestion: Continue propagating the [module] prefix pattern to remaining client-side error logs.
issues.summary: Some client-side error logs in non-route-handler code lack [module] prefix
evidence: While all route handlers now use [module] prefix, some hooks and client-side code still use bare console.error without module prefix.

issues.suggestion: Consider routing webhook agent SSE through Go streaming proxy for consistency.
issues.summary: Some streaming orchestration exists in both web API and streaming proxy
evidence: The webhook dispatch route handles SSE stream reading and token relaying, overlapping with Go streaming proxy responsibility.

issues.suggestion: Consider using a class or context-based approach for socket lifecycle management.
issues.summary: socket.ts uses module-level singleton state
evidence: Socket and currentToken are module-level singletons coupling connection lifecycle to module initialization.

issues.suggestion: Extract SSE parsing into a reusable helper with typed event shapes.
issues.summary: Webhook dispatch handler has inline SSE parsing logic
evidence: The SSE stream reading loop in dispatch/route.ts parses data lines inline with try/catch and string manipulation.

issues.suggestion: Extract SSE line parsing into a typed helper function.
issues.summary: SSE stream parsing uses inline string manipulation
evidence: The dispatch route handler parses SSE lines inline with string.startsWith and JSON.parse rather than a dedicated parser.

issues.suggestion: Consider splitting into separate providers for server data, permissions, and routing.
issues.summary: ChatProvider still manages several concerns in a single component
evidence: ChatProvider handles server list, channel list, members, agents, permissions, unread state, and routing in one component, though complexity has been reduced via fetchAndSet.

issues.suggestion: Consider moving e2e tests to a top-level tests/ directory if the project grows.
issues.summary: E2E test helpers are inside the web package
evidence: The e2e/ directory lives inside packages/web rather than at the monorepo root.

issues.suggestion: Add type narrowing to simpler route handlers where body parsing is needed.
issues.summary: Some simpler route handlers still use unvalidated request body parsing
evidence: While the most complex route handlers now use zod, some simpler ones still parse request.json() without type narrowing.
